sprint: Identity Gate Full Redesign -- Root-Caused Scrollbar, Page-Owned Authentication, Lumo as Gatekeeper, Per-Star Colours
task: A direct follow-up with real user-screenshot evidence the prior scrollbar fix hadn't fully held: 'the scroll is still there. the size can still be increase. let the page own the authentication visually. bring companion as gatekeeper. make the entire page feel like night sky. for each star clicked in grid use different color.' Root-caused the real scrollbar source via direct scrollWidth/clientWidth measurement -- a selected tap-grid star's transform:scale()+glow box-shadow extends its painted bounds past its layout box, which Chromium counts toward an ancestor's scrollable overflow even though it never affects layout, compounded by CSS silently coupling an unset overflow-x to auto the moment overflow-y isn't visible. Fixed by wrapping the board in a new overflow:hidden .magic-card-tapgrid-boardwrap (with padding to absorb the glow bloom) and adding explicit overflow-x:hidden on the overlay and panel -- verified zero horizontal overflow at every tested viewport height (480-900px) with a real glowing selected star. Widened the Gate's own panel (520px->680px) and tap board (320px->min(460px,92vw,48vh)) for 'the size can still increase.' Added a .magic-card-gate-panel--challenge modifier that sheds the panel's boxed chrome specifically during the actual tap challenge, so it reads as sitting on the night sky rather than a modal floating on it. Added a purely decorative, background-image-only .magic-card-starfield behind every Magic Card screen for 'the entire page feels like a night sky' -- deliberately not per-star DOM/transform elements, so it can never re-trigger the exact bug just fixed. Added a Gatekeeper header (Lumo's real portrait + name + line) atop every tap challenge, chosen as a fixed figure across Continue/tile-tap/Recall per Companion Canon V2's own Guardian framing. Gave each tapped star, and each connecting line segment, its own distinct colour from an 8-hue palette cycling by tap order via a new --star-color custom property, recomputed live on every selection change. Verified via the full existing pattern-challenge suite plus the entire Magic Card/Companion Canon/Story Egg regression suite and both goldenBuild.js suites, all passing unchanged, plus a real screenshot. Confined to css/style.css and js/magicCardUI.js.
